message:
fix(btrfs): reject zero-length names in INODE_REF/DIR_ITEM/XATTR_ITEM (bd-apy2u)

Btrfs name-bearing items (inode refs, directory entries, xattrs) all use
name_len-prefixed payloads, but the kernel never produces zero-length
names for these keys — they are name-bearing by definition. The parsers
in `ffs-btrfs` previously accepted name_len=0 for all three kinds, which
allowed adversarial / corrupted images to pass through validation with
empty names that downstream code is not prepared to handle (and which
break round-trip with any sensible encoder anyway).

Changes:

* `parse_inode_refs` (crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs): reject `name_len == 0`
  with `ParseError::InvalidField { field: "inode_ref.name_len",
  reason: "must be non-zero" }` before the bounds-check loop.
* `parse_dir_items` (same file): reject `name_len == 0` for DIR_ITEM, in
  addition to the existing `data_len != 0` rejection (DIR_ITEM never
  carries a data payload — that shape is XATTR_ITEM).
* `parse_xattr_items` (same file): reject `name_len == 0`, while keeping
  the existing acceptance of zero-length *values* (legitimate xattrs).
* `btrfs_name_len_u16` shared encoder helper: reject `len == 0` so the
  inverse `try_to_bytes` paths for `BtrfsInodeRef` / `BtrfsDirItem` /
  `BtrfsXattrItem` cannot encode something the parser would reject —
  preserves parse∘encode = identity on the legal subset.

Tests:

* Inline tests in `crates/ffs-btrfs/src/lib.rs` cover the new rejection
  branch for each of the three parsers and for the encoder paths
  (`inode_ref_try_to_bytes_rejects_empty_name`,
  `dir_item_try_to_bytes_rejects_empty_name`, plus the existing
  parse-side coverage extended to the empty-name case).
* Conformance goldens in `crates/ffs-btrfs/tests/conformance_ondisk_golden.rs`
  add `dir_item_zero_name_len_rejected`, `inode_ref_zero_name_len_rejected`,
  and `xattr_item_zero_name_len_rejected`, asserting both the parse-side
  rejection on a 30-byte / 10-byte all-zero payload and (where an encoder
  exists) the encode-side guard.

Docs:

* `FEATURE_PARITY.md`: btrfs item payload decode row updated to mention
  `parse_inode_refs` / `parse_xattr_items` and the new zero-length name
  rejection invariant for all three name-bearing item kinds.
* `conformance/COVERAGE.md`: bumps DirItem (5→6) and adds new BtrfsInodeRef
  (1) and BtrfsXattrItem (1) MUST-clause rows; btrfs total moves
  79/79 → 82/82. Adversarial regression summary line for the item
  payload parsers grows the "zero-length name rejection for
  INODE_REF/DIR_ITEM/XATTR_ITEM" entry.

Beads:

* `.beads/issues.jsonl`: introduces bd-apy2u "Reject empty btrfs
  name-bearing item payloads" in `in_progress` (assignee cod-fs,
  labels btrfs/conformance) — left in_progress here for the
  spec-first conformance proof to land before close.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -48,7 +48,7 @@
 | btrfs internal node parsing | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | `parse_internal_items` in `ffs-ondisk` |
 | btrfs sys_chunk mapping | `fs/btrfs/volumes.c` | ✅ | `map_logical_to_physical` in `ffs-ondisk`, with proptest + metamorphic coverage in `crates/ffs-ondisk/src/btrfs.rs` for translation covariance, chunk-order permutation invariance, and Single-vs-DUP primary mapping equivalence. |
 | btrfs read-only tree walk | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | `walk_tree` in `ffs-btrfs` |
-| btrfs item payload decode (ROOT/INODE/DIR/EXTENT_DATA) | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-btrfs` (`parse_root_item`, `parse_root_ref`, `parse_inode_item`, `parse_dir_items`, `parse_extent_data`) with direct cargo-fuzz coverage in `fuzz_btrfs_tree_items` for deterministic decode and field-level payload invariants. `parse_root_item()` reads level/UUID fields from the packed kernel layout, invalidates UUID-era fields when `generation_v2` is stale, rejects impossible tree levels, and rejects partial extension-field fragments; `parse_root_ref()` rejects ROOT_REF payloads with trailing bytes after the declared name; `parse_dir_items()` rejects nonzero `data_len` payloads that belong to xattr-shaped items instead of directory entries; `parse_inode_item()` rejects payloads that are not the fixed 160-byte `btrfs_inode_item` size and rejects impossible timestamp nanoseconds before they reach `Duration`/`SystemTime` conversion paths; and `parse_extent_data()` rejects unsupported extent compression values, nonzero encryption/other-encoding fields, and trailing bytes after fixed regular/prealloc payloads instead of treating encoded or overlong payloads as raw readable data. |
+| btrfs item payload decode (ROOT/INODE/DIR/EXTENT_DATA) | `fs/btrfs/ctree.c` | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-btrfs` (`parse_root_item`, `parse_root_ref`, `parse_inode_refs`, `parse_inode_item`, `parse_dir_items`, `parse_xattr_items`, `parse_extent_data`) with direct cargo-fuzz coverage in `fuzz_btrfs_tree_items` for deterministic decode and field-level payload invariants. `parse_root_item()` reads level/UUID fields from the packed kernel layout, invalidates UUID-era fields when `generation_v2` is stale, rejects impossible tree levels, and rejects partial extension-field fragments; `parse_root_ref()` rejects ROOT_REF payloads with trailing bytes after the declared name; `parse_inode_refs()`, `parse_dir_items()`, and `parse_xattr_items()` reject zero-length names; `parse_dir_items()` rejects nonzero `data_len` payloads that belong to xattr-shaped items instead of directory entries; `parse_inode_item()` rejects payloads that are not the fixed 160-byte `btrfs_inode_item` size and rejects impossible timestamp nanoseconds before they reach `Duration`/`SystemTime` conversion paths; and `parse_extent_data()` rejects unsupported extent compression values, nonzero encryption/other-encoding fields, and trailing bytes after fixed regular/prealloc payloads instead of treating encoded or overlong payloads as raw readable data. |
 | btrfs open/validate pipeline | `fs/btrfs/disk-io.c` | ✅ | `BtrfsContext` in `ffs-core` |
 | btrfs transaction parity | `fs/btrfs/transaction.c` | ✅ | Transaction begin/commit/abort semantics and integration paths are implemented with coverage in unit + E2E suites. |
 | btrfs delayed refs parity | `fs/btrfs/delayed-ref.c` | ✅ | `DelayedRefQueue` + `BtrfsRef` model + bounded flush/refcount tracking in `ffs-btrfs::BtrfsExtentAllocator`, with queue/refcount/stress tests. The allocator now also exposes fragmentation-aware largest-free-extent discovery from block-group/extent-tree gaps, and `OpenFs::largest_contiguous_free_run` uses that value for btrfs instead of reporting total free bytes as a conservative upper bound. |

@@ -40,11 +40,13 @@
 | BtrfsDevItem | ✅ | ✅ | 7 | 7 | 100% | 1TB device with 512GB used plus invalid ID/accounting rejection |
 | BtrfsRootItem | ✅ | ✅ | 8 | 8 | 100% | via roottree_leaf fixture plus fixed-offset level/UUID layout, stale generation_v2 invalidation, impossible level rejection, and partial extension-field rejection |
 | BtrfsRootRef | ✅ | ✅ | 3 | 3 | 100% | parse-only root refs with exact payload length, short-name rejection, and trailing-byte rejection |
+| BtrfsInodeRef | ✅ | ✅ | 1 | 1 | 100% | zero-length name rejection plus encoder guard |
 | BtrfsInodeItem | ✅ | ✅ | 8 | 8 | 100% | via fstree_leaf fixture plus timestamp nanosecond bound rejection and fixed-size trailing-byte rejection |
-| BtrfsDirItem | ✅ | ✅ | 5 | 5 | 100% | via fstree_leaf fixture plus nonzero data_len rejection |
+| BtrfsDirItem | ✅ | ✅ | 6 | 6 | 100% | via fstree_leaf fixture plus nonzero data_len rejection and zero-length name rejection |
+| BtrfsXattrItem | ✅ | ✅ | 1 | 1 | 100% | zero-length name rejection while zero-length values remain accepted |
 | BtrfsExtentData | ✅ | ✅ | 6 | 6 | 100% | via fstree_leaf fixture plus fixed-size regular payload trailing-byte rejection |
 
-**btrfs Total: 79 MUST clauses, 79 passing = 100.0%**
+**btrfs Total: 82 MUST clauses, 82 passing = 100.0%**
 
 ## Priority Gaps
 
@@ -68,7 +70,7 @@ None. All identified on-disk structures have conformance fixtures.
 | Btrfs chunk-tree items | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover valid multi-stripe chunk-tree item parsing, fixed header truncation, declared stripe payload truncation, trailing payload rejection after declared stripes, zero chunk length, zero stripe length, zero stripes, zero stripe device IDs, and multiple RAID profile bit rejection |
 | Btrfs dev items | ✅ | ✅ | synthetic adversarial seeds cover full field-layout parsing, max numeric/classification values, trailing bytes after the fixed 98-byte item, zero device ID rejection, zero-capacity device rejection, impossible `bytes_used > total_bytes` accounting, and truncated payload rejection |
 | Btrfs superblocks | ✅ | ✅ | synthetic adversarial seeds cover valid superblock-region parsing, image-offset parsing, bad magic, zero and non-power-of-two sizing fields, oversized sector/node/stripe sizing fields, zero-capacity, zero-device, and `bytes_used > total_bytes` accounting rejection, unsupported checksum types, oversized sys_chunk_array declarations, invalid root/chunk/log tree levels, and short region/image rejection |
-| Btrfs item payload parsers | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover root/root_ref/inode/dir/xattr/extent payload valid boundaries, multi-entry dir/xattr payloads, short headers, length overflows, ROOT_ITEM partial extension-field rejection, nonzero DIR_ITEM data_len rejection, fixed-size INODE_ITEM trailing-byte rejection, fixed-size regular EXTENT_DATA trailing-byte rejection, unsupported extent types, compression values, encoding fields, invalid inode timestamp nanoseconds, zero root bytenr rejection, and malformed ROOT_REF fallback behavior for subvolumes/snapshots |
+| Btrfs item payload parsers | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover root/root_ref/inode/dir/xattr/extent payload valid boundaries, multi-entry dir/xattr payloads, short headers, length overflows, zero-length name rejection for INODE_REF/DIR_ITEM/XATTR_ITEM, ROOT_ITEM partial extension-field rejection, nonzero DIR_ITEM data_len rejection, fixed-size INODE_ITEM trailing-byte rejection, fixed-size regular EXTENT_DATA trailing-byte rejection, unsupported extent types, compression values, encoding fields, invalid inode timestamp nanoseconds, zero root bytenr rejection, and malformed ROOT_REF fallback behavior for subvolumes/snapshots |
 | Btrfs send streams | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover command CRC32C validation, missing-END rejection, unknown command fallback, zero-length and unknown attributes, multi-attribute commands, trailing partial command headers, and END payload TLV validation |
 | Btrfs delayed refs | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial regression tests cover zero-limit flush preservation, bounded partial flush accounting, delete-underflow queue preservation, and sequence-ordered drain across extent key order |
 | Btrfs transactions | ✅ | ✅ | deterministic adversarial mutation tests cover same-tree root replacement, delayed-ref commit failure nonvisibility, and tree-root address overflow nonvisibility |
